text: Read the following and answer. Ten students - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I and J - represent their college in four sports: tennis, badminton, table tennis and snooker. The badminton team has one student less than the tennis team. (i) A, B and C are not tennis players. (ii) D, E and F are not in the badminton team. (iii) G, H and I are not in the table tennis team. (iv) J is a table tennis player. (v) No student is a snooker player. Each student plays exactly one sport. Which of the following students could be table tennis players?
explanation: Table tennis can only include A,B,C,D,E,F,J (G,H,I excluded). A TT size of 3 forces tennis=4, badminton=3 (integer sizes); a size of 4 would need tennis=3.5 (impossible). Only 'J, C and F' (size 3 of allowed letters) is feasible. Option (a) is out because G cannot play TT.

text: Read the following and answer. Ten students - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I and J - represent their college in four sports: tennis, badminton, table tennis and snooker. The badminton team has one student less than the tennis team. (i) A, B and C are not tennis players. (ii) D, E and F are not in the badminton team. (iii) G, H and I are not in the table tennis team. (iv) J is a table tennis player. (v) No student is a snooker player. Each student plays exactly one sport. What is the largest possible number of students who could be table tennis players?
explanation: TT can only be A,B,C,D,E,F,J = 7 students. Taking TT=7 forces tennis=2, badminton=1 (since 11-2T=7 gives T=2), and G,H,I fill those two teams. So the maximum is 7.

text: Read the following and answer. Ten students - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I and J - represent their college in four sports: tennis, badminton, table tennis and snooker. The badminton team has one student less than the tennis team. (i) A, B and C are not tennis players. (ii) D, E and F are not in the badminton team. (iii) G, H and I are not in the table tennis team. (iv) J is a table tennis player. (v) No student is a snooker player. Each student plays exactly one sport. If no student except I is in the badminton team, which students could be tennis players?
explanation: Badminton = {I} so tennis = 2, TT = 7. G and H cannot play TT or badminton, so both must be the tennis players: G and H.

text: Read the following and answer. Ten students - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I and J - represent their college in four sports: tennis, badminton, table tennis and snooker. The badminton team has one student less than the tennis team. (i) A, B and C are not tennis players. (ii) D, E and F are not in the badminton team. (iii) G, H and I are not in the table tennis team. (iv) J is a table tennis player. (v) No student is a snooker player. Each student plays exactly one sport. If the table tennis team has only one member, who must be a tennis player?
explanation: TT = {J} (size 1) forces tennis=5, badminton=4. D, E, F cannot be in badminton or this TT, so they must be tennis. Hence D must be a tennis player.

text: Read the following and answer. Ten students - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I and J - represent their college in four sports: tennis, badminton, table tennis and snooker. The badminton team has one student less than the tennis team. (i) A, B and C are not tennis players. (ii) D, E and F are not in the badminton team. (iii) G, H and I are not in the table tennis team. (iv) J is a table tennis player. (v) No student is a snooker player. Each student plays exactly one sport. If G, H and C are the only badminton players, which three must be the only table tennis players?
explanation: Badminton = {G,H,C} (size 3) forces tennis=4, TT=3. A and B cannot be tennis and badminton is full, so they join J in table tennis: A, B and J.

text: Read the following and answer. Ten students - A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I and J - represent their college in four sports: tennis, badminton, table tennis and snooker. The badminton team has one student less than the tennis team. (i) A, B and C are not tennis players. (ii) D, E and F are not in the badminton team. (iii) G, H and I are not in the table tennis team. (iv) J is a table tennis player. (v) No student is a snooker player. Each student plays exactly one sport. If A and G are the only badminton players, how many students must be in the table tennis team?
explanation: Badminton = 2 forces tennis = 3 and table tennis = 11 - 2(3) = 5.

text: There are 10 packets of candies (#1 to #10), each containing 'x' candies. In 9 packets each candy weighs 10 grams; in one packet each candy weighs 20 grams. Using a weighing machine that shows exact weight, you must find the heavier packet. If x = 9, what is the minimum number of weighings needed?
explanation: Take 0,1,2,...,9 candies from the ten packets (max 9 = x). The excess over the all-10g weight equals 10 x (count from the heavy packet), uniquely identifying it in a single weighing.

text: There are 10 packets of candies (#1 to #10), each containing 'x' candies. In 9 packets each candy weighs 10 grams; in one packet each candy weighs 20 grams. Using a weighing machine that shows exact weight, you must find the heavier packet. If x = 7, what is the minimum number of weighings needed?
explanation: One weighing can distinguish only x+1 = 8 packets (counts 0..7), fewer than 10. Two weighings (8x8 = 64 codes) are enough, so the minimum is 2.

text: There are 10 packets of candies (#1 to #10), each containing 'x' candies. In 9 packets each candy weighs 10 grams; in one packet each candy weighs 20 grams. Using a weighing machine that shows exact weight, you must find the heavier packet. If x = 3, what is the minimum number of weighings needed?
explanation: With counts 0..3 (4 values), one weighing distinguishes 4 packets; two weighings give 4x4 = 16 >= 10. Minimum is 2.

text: There are 10 packets of candies (#1 to #10), each containing 'x' candies. In 9 packets each candy weighs 10 grams; in one packet each candy weighs 20 grams. Using a weighing machine that shows exact weight, you must find the heavier packet. If x = 2, what is the minimum number of weighings needed?
explanation: With counts 0..2 (3 values), each weighing distinguishes 3 outcomes. 3^2 = 9 < 10 but 3^3 = 27 >= 10, so 3 weighings are needed.

text: Use the redefined operators: '-' means x (multiply), 'x' means / (divide), '/' means + (add), '+' means - (subtract). Calculate: 5 + 8 - 25 x 5 / 40
Use the redefined operators: '-' means x, 'x' means /, '/' means +, '+' means -. With which statement can you obtain the value 8?
Use the redefined operators: '-' means x, 'x' means /, '/' means +, '+' means -. Which of the following is true?
explanation: Option C: 6 - 4 x 2 / 2 + 1 = 6 - 4 + 1 = 3, which matches its right side.

text: Use the redefined operators: '-' means x, 'x' means /, '/' means +, '+' means -. Calculate: (1 + 2 - 3 x 1 / 4) / (4 + 5 - 4 x 2 / 1)
Use the redefined operators: '-' means x, 'x' means /, '/' means +, '+' means -. Find the value of: (4 + 3 - 5 x 1 / 4) x (5 + 2 - 3 x 1 / 4)
explanation: First bracket: 4 - 3 x 5 / 1 + 4 = 4 - 15 + 4 = -7. Second: 5 - 2 x 3 / 1 + 4 = 5 - 6 + 4 = 3. The connecting 'x' becomes '/': -7 / 3 = -7/3.

text: In a certain language, NOIDA is coded as OPJEB. How is DELHI coded in that language?
explanation: Each letter shifts +1 (N->O, O->P, I->J, D->E, A->B). DELHI -> EFMIJ.

text: If HOT is coded as GNS, how is COLD coded?
explanation: Each letter shifts -1 (H->G, O->N, T->S). COLD -> BNKC.

text: In a certain code, TREE is written as UQFD. How is LEAF written in that code?
explanation: Letters shift +1, -1, +1, -1 (T->U, R->Q, E->F, E->D). LEAF -> M, D, B, E = MDBE.
